Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jun-06-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ICONIQ CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, LLC'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs.

Defendant's motion for attorney fees and costs is granted in the combined amount of $500,000.

This case regarded a finder's fee agreement. It went from filing to summary judgment in less than a year. Counsel (and the court) are commended for that. Defendant is the prevailing party and is due contractual attorney fees and costs.

Papers on the initial fee motion (and this one) joust about who did what to whom during the litigation. I thus requested, and received, daily billing records of defendant's counsel. Having read those records, and based on 36 years of litigation experience, I find the hours expended and the work performed to be appropriate. (Serrano v. Priest (1977) 20 Cal.3d 25, 49 ("experienced trial judge is the best judge of the value of professional services rendered in his court"). Not every litigation battle must be won to prevail in the war.

I am less comfortable with the hourly rates sought. Defense counsel seek as much as $1,425 an hour (partner), $985 (senior associate) and $685 (junior associate). These rates are well above the San Francisco mean even in the report defendant relies on (Appendix Ex. 3) - and above the rates I regularly see in fee motions.

In sum, $500,000 is reasonable for attorney fees and costs in this case.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-01-2021 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ICONIQ CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Iconiq Capital Management, LLC's demurrers to the first amended complaint (FAC) are overruled.

The FAC adequately pleads a cause of action for breach of contract. The kind of specific pleading defendant desires is not required in California courts; "a complaint is adequate so long as it apprises the defendant of the factual basis for the claim." (Prue v. Brady Co./San Diego, Inc. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 1367, 1376.)

Defendant's factual disputation is impermissible at the pleading stage.

The FAC also adequately pleads a cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. This claim may be pled in the alternative to contract breach.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964 (302/RBU)
